sense.definition: a fine powder made from almonds, used as a gluten-free flour substitute or protein-rich ingredient in baking

examples.x: He prefers a guilt-free chocolate chip cookie made with almond meal.
They prepared a flavorful and gluten-free crust for their homemade quiche using almond meal.
You can create a wholesome breakfast by combining almond meal with oats.

sense.definition: the outer layer of a cereal grain, rich in fiber and nutrients

examples.x: She mixed bran into her homemade muffin batter, picturing herself as a creative baker.
You can enhance your smoothie by adding a spoonful of bran.

sense.definition: a nutritious and gluten-free pseudo-cereal with small, protein-rich grains

examples.x: As they explored a local farmers market, they discovered a bag of amaranth.

sense.definition: a small, nutrient-dense seed rich in omega-3 fatty acids and dietary fiber

examples.x: They mixed flax into their homemade granola bars.
You can incorporate flax into your favorite salad dressing.

sense.definition: a small and nutrient-rich seed derived from the flax plant, known for its omega-3 fatty acids, fiber, and potential health benefits
a small and nutritious seed of the flax plant, used for its oil content and potential health benefits

examples.x: My friend uses linseed to make homemade granola bars for herself.

sense.definition: a small seed of the sesame plant, known for its rich nutty flavor and versatile culinary use
a gluten-free grain with a nutty taste, often used in cooking and baking as either whole grains or flour

examples.x: We enjoyed a delightful buckwheat salad with roasted vegetables.

sense.definition: a type of ancient wheat with a nutty flavor and a high nutritional value

examples.x: He was surprised when he tasted the emmer pudding for the first time.
She prepared a vibrant emmer salad with roasted vegetables.

sense.definition: an ancient grain with a mild, nutty flavor and a chewy texture

examples.x: You can add cooked spelt to your favorite grain bowl.

sense.definition: a type of dark and hard wheat that is grown in dry regions, used to make pasta

examples.x: They enjoyed a comforting durum soup on a chilly evening.
You can use durum flour to make homemade bread.

sense.definition: the common grain that is used in making flour, taken from a cereal grass which is green and tall

examples.x: The recipe called for wheat to be ground into flour for making bread.
They included wheat in their diet for its nutritional benefits and fiber content.
The farmer harvested wheat from the fields to produce flour for local bakeries.
He avoided products containing wheat due to his gluten sensitivity.

sense.definition: the whole, unprocessed kernel of the wheat grain

examples.x: She blended soaked wheat berries into her favorite smoothie.
They added cooked wheat berries to their homemade granola bars.

sense.definition: a cereal grass grown in cold climates with grains that are used in making whiskey, bread or animal food

examples.x: We gathered around the table for a traditional German dinner, enjoying hearty rye dumplings alongside savory sausages.

sense.definition: a cereal grain used as food for humans and animals and for making alcoholic beverages

examples.x: I asked my mother to prepare a refreshing barley salad for me.
The brewery sourced its barley from local farms to ensure freshness.

sense.definition: a type of grain that grows on a plant in cool climates, used as food for people and animals

examples.x: I made myself a delicious oat pancake stack.

sense.definition: a drought-tolerant grain with small round grains that possesses a mildly nutty flavor and a slightly chewy texture

examples.x: It was a busy day, but I managed to make a quick and tasty pearl millet stir-fry.
While I was looking for a way to distract myself, I decided to bake a batch of pearl millet and cranberry muffins.

sense.definition: small seeds of a large crop that grows in warm regions, used to feed birds or make flour

examples.x: It was a cold winter evening, but I warmed up with a comforting bowl of millet and butternut squash soup.

sense.definition: a long-grain aquatic grass seed that is known for its nutty flavor and chewy texture

examples.x: He cooked a delicious wild rice and mushroom risotto for his vegetarian dinner.
She enjoyed a simple yet satisfying meal of roasted chicken and wild rice.

sense.definition: a small, gluten-free grain native to Ethiopia and Eritrea

examples.x: The chef prepared a mouthwatering teff stir-fry, incorporating flavorful spices and vegetables.
They mixed teff grains into their homemade granola bars, creating a delightful crunch and nutty flavor.

sense.definition: a nutritious grain known for its small, finger-like grains and gluten-free properties

examples.x: She packed a nutritious lunchbox for her children, including finger millet crackers with a creamy hummus dip.
She prepared a warm bowl of creamy finger millet porridge for herself.

sense.definition: a tall plant growing in Central America that produces yellow seeds, which are used in cooking

examples.x: As the summer sun set, a family gathered in the backyard, grilling maize skewers alongside juicy grilled meats.
In the school garden, the students proudly harvested the maize they had planted.

sense.definition: the dried grains or kernels of a cereal plant, commonly used in various culinary preparations

examples.x: As the corn dried in the sun, it turned into golden kernels ready for harvest.
I crushed the corn into coarse pieces to use as a topping for my cornbread.

sense.definition: a grain crop widely cultivated for its edible seeds and used in various food and industrial applications

examples.x: He marinated chicken in a tangy sorghum glaze before grilling it to perfection.
They enjoyed a slice of warm sorghum bread with a dollop of butter as a brunch.

sense.definition: a type of grain commonly used in Asian cuisine and known for their distinctive tear-shaped appearance
a type of flour that has been chemically treated to whiten its color and soften its texture

examples.x: For a quick snack, she coated chicken strips in seasoned bleached flour before frying them to golden perfection.
They substituted bleached flour in their cookie recipe to achieve a softer and tender texture.
We followed a traditional bread recipe, combining yeast, water, and bleached flour for a classic loaf.

sense.definition: a type of flour that has been fortified with additional nutrients, such as vitamins and minerals

examples.x: He mixed enriched flour with yeast and warm water to make homemade dinner rolls.
She used enriched flour to bake a batch of fluffy chocolate chip cookies.

sense.definition: a finely milled type of flour that is low in protein content

examples.x: For her grandmother's birthday, she baked a moist red velvet cake using cake flour.
They used cake flour in their recipe to create a delicate and fluffy sponge for their layered cake.

sense.definition: a type of flour with a moderate protein content that is finer than all-purpose flour but coarser than cake flour

examples.x: He carefully folded the fruit filling into the pastry dough made with pastry flour.
He mixed pastry flour with water and yeast to create a soft and chewy pizza dough.

sense.definition: flour without baking powder, yeast or any other substance that makes cakes or baked goods rise

examples.x: He added plain flour to the sauce, thickening it to a creamy consistency for the pasta dish.
I measured out the precise amount of plain flour needed for the cake recipe I was following.

sense.definition: a versatile type of flour with a moderate protein content that is commonly used in a wide range of culinary applications

examples.x: He combined all-purpose flour with buttermilk and eggs to make a fluffy pancake batter for breakfast.
I added a spoonful of all-purpose flour to the simmering soup, thickening it slightly.

sense.definition: a type of flour that already contains leavening agents, such as baking powder, making it suitable for recipes that require a rise or lift

examples.x: He followed the recipe and combined self-raising flour with butter and sugar to make delicious cookies.
He grabbed the self-raising flour from the pantry to bake fluffy pancakes for breakfast.

sense.definition: a coarse whole wheat flour made from the endosperm of the wheat kernel

examples.x: As a vegan alternative, they used graham flour to create a plant-based pie crust.
He baked a loaf of bread using graham flour, giving it a rich and nutty flavor.
She decided to use graham flour instead of regular flour to make her homemade graham crackers.

sense.definition: a type of flour that has not been chemically treated to whiten its color or soften its texture

examples.x: After learning about the benefits of unbleached flour, he made a conscious effort to incorporate it into his everyday cooking.
I found that using unbleached flour in my pancakes resulted in a more flavorful and satisfying breakfast.
She noticed a difference in taste and texture when she switched to unbleached flour in her cookie recipe.

sense.definition: a high-protein flour used to improve dough elasticity and structure in baking

examples.x: They were hosting a barbecue and decided to prepare homemade veggie burgers using gluten flour.

sense.definition: small pieces of crushed durum or similar wheat grains used in making pasta and pudding

examples.x: She used semolina to make homemade pasta from scratch.
The recipe called for semolina to give the bread a unique texture.
He sprinkled semolina on the baking tray to prevent the bread from sticking.
The cake was made with semolina, giving it a slightly grainy consistency.
Semolina is a key ingredient in traditional Italian desserts like semolina pudding.

sense.definition: a type of flour that does not contain gluten, a protein found in wheat, barley, and rye

examples.x: I mixed gluten-free flour with a variety of spices to create a flavorful coating for my crispy gluten-free fried chicken.
They hosted a brunch and prepared gluten-free pancakes using a blend of gluten-free flours.

sense.definition: high-protein flour used for bread making and recipes needing strong gluten development

examples.x: Hard flour, known for its high protein content, is ideal for baking breads that require a strong structure and a chewy texture.
Unlike regular flour, hard flour has a higher gluten content, which gives it the ability to hold its shape during baking.

sense.definition: high-protein flour for making bread with a strong gluten network and chewy texture

examples.x: The bread flour helped the dough rise beautifully, resulting in a loaf with a lofty height.
With its higher protein content, bread flour gives breads a stronger structure and a chewier texture.

sense.definition: the flour made by grinding wheat grains, commonly used in various culinary applications

examples.x: She mixed wheat flour, water, and yeast to make a dough for homemade bread.

sense.definition: (of bread or flour) containing whole grains of wheat and also the husk

examples.x: He found whole wheat crackers to be the perfect snack option.

sense.definition: (of bread or flour) containing whole grains of wheat and also the husk

examples.x: She baked a batch of wholesome wholemeal cookies, combining oats, nuts, and dried fruits for a chewy and nutritious treat.
They enjoyed a satisfying wholemeal pizza, with a thin and crispy crust made from wholemeal flour.

sense.definition: an unbleached flour that is made by grinding whole grains of wheat

examples.x: She added a spoonful of wheatmeal to her morning oatmeal for an extra boost of fiber and nutrients.

sense.definition: a starch extracted from the cassava root, used in cooking and baking for its thickening properties

examples.x: The chef used tapioca flour to make crispy and delicious gluten-free cookies.

sense.definition: flattened oat groats that have been processed into flakes, often used as a versatile and nutritious ingredient in breakfast cereals

examples.x: I toasted a handful of rolled oats with maple syrup and spices to make a crunchy and sweet granola topping.
They created a wholesome energy bar by combining rolled oats, nuts, seeds, and a drizzle of honey.

sense.definition: a starchy powder derived from the rhizomes of tropical plants, used as a thickening agent in cooking and baking

examples.x: He used arrowroot as a gluten-free alternative to wheat flour when baking cookies.
I added arrowroot starch to my homemade soup to thicken the broth.
I sprinkled arrowroot powder on fresh fruits to make a delicious and healthy dessert topping.
She mixed arrowroot powder with water to create a homemade facial mask.

sense.definition: a whole grain rice variety with the bran intact, offering nutty flavor and chewy texture

examples.x: I like to mix cooked brown rice with black beans, corn, and salsa for a flavorful and filling burrito filling.
She cooks brown rice in a rice cooker and serves it alongside grilled chicken for a balanced meal.

sense.definition: a cracked and parboiled wheat grain commonly used in Middle Eastern cuisine

examples.x: I like to use bulgur in my vegetarian chili recipe for added texture and flavor.
She prepared a delicious stuffed pepper dish using bulgur, vegetables, and spices.

sense.definition: any plant that is produced for grains that can be eaten or used in making flour

examples.x: I start my day with a bowl of cereal and a splash of cold milk.
They use cereal as a crunchy topping for their homemade ice cream sundaes.

sense.definition: a breakfast cereal made with roasted flakes of maize flour that is usually eaten with milk and sugar

examples.x: He remembers childhood memories of enjoying a bowl of cornflakes while watching cartoons on Saturday mornings.

sense.definition: fine white starch of maize, used in cooking to thicken sauces or soups

examples.x: She dusts the chicken wings with cornflour before frying them.
She uses cornflour to thicken her homemade fruit jam, achieving the perfect consistency for spreading on toast.

sense.definition: grain that is ground into a powder, used for feeding animals or making flour

examples.x: In their sustainable farming practice, they use the meal as compost, enriching the soil for future crops.
She attends a workshop on animal nutrition, learning about the importance of incorporating meal into livestock diets.
She mixes the meal with water to form a thick paste, feeding it to her pet rabbits.

sense.definition: a thick, soft food from ground oats, eaten usually for breakfast

examples.x: He likes to add a dollop of peanut butter to his oatmeal for a protein boost.
She starts her day with a warm bowl of oatmeal topped with fresh berries.

sense.definition: a thin, translucent sheet made from rice flour and water, used in various Asian cuisines for wrapping spring rolls

examples.x: As the rice paper softened, I carefully layered sliced mangoes and coconut flakes, creating a sweet and tropical dessert roll.
He dipped the rice paper in warm water to soften it before filling it with shrimp and herbs.

sense.definition: a young corn with soft kernels that is high in sugar, grown on a maize plant, used in cooking

examples.x: As the summer harvest arrived, the farmers' market overflowed with fresh sweet corn.
As the sun set, we roasted sweet corn on the open fire.
I boiled the sweet corn until tender, then served it with a dollop of herb-infused butter for a simple and satisfying meal.

sense.definition: the starch or flour of dried manioc or cassava root
the seed of an oriental plant that is rich in protein, used for making oil or food

examples.x: She mixed cooked soya beans with vegetables and spices to make flavorful and nutritious veggie burgers.
She roasted soya beans in the oven with a sprinkle of chili powder for a spicy and crunchy party snack.
